name: Louisiana
agency: Louisiana Secretary of State, Commercial Division

llc.formName: Affidavit to Dissolve Louisiana Limited Liability Company
steps: Vote to dissolve the LLC per your operating agreement
File all delinquent annual reports and pay outstanding fees
Wind up business affairs and settle all debts and obligations
File final tax returns with Louisiana Department of Revenue
Complete the Affidavit to Dissolve (requires notarization)
File electronically or by mail with $75 filing fee
Notify creditors and claimants of dissolution
Cancel any licenses, permits, and trade names
llc.notes: The affidavit requires signatures from all managers or members (depending on management structure) and must be acknowledged (notarized). If any incorporator/shareholder prohibits the dissolution, the affidavit will not be processed.

steps: Obtain board of directors resolution authorizing dissolution
Obtain shareholder approval of dissolution (requires unanimous consent or per charter)
File all delinquent annual reports and pay outstanding fees
Wind up business affairs and settle all obligations
File final tax returns with Louisiana Department of Revenue
Prepare and file Articles of Dissolution with $60 filing fee
Notify creditors and claimants of dissolution
Cancel any licenses, permits, and trade names
corporation.notes: Corporation dissolution requires filing Articles of Dissolution with the Commercial Division. Louisiana requires all delinquent annual reports to be filed before dissolution will be accepted.

tax.notes: File final corporate income and franchise tax returns with the Louisiana Department of Revenue. Must also file final sales tax returns and withholding returns. Obtain tax clearance before filing dissolution. A corporation must dissolve its charter with the Secretary of State and file its final franchise tax return.

requirements.publicationDetails: Must publish notice of dissolution at least once in a newspaper of general circulation in the parish where the registered office is located.
requirements.employeeNotification: Provide WARN Act notice if applicable (60 days for 100+ employees)
requirements.creditorNotification: Must notify known creditors in writing; Louisiana law requires reasonable notice to creditors
requirements.registeredAgentNotes: Must be current on all annual reports and filings before dissolution will be accepted
requirements.windingUpPeriod: Entity continues to exist for purpose of winding up after dissolution
requirements.boardOrMemberApproval: LLC: All managers or members must sign the affidavit. Corporation: Board resolution followed by shareholder approval per articles of incorporation.

includes: Every required form with direct download links
